blocks.body: Possessive Forms — Possessive forms before a noun and on their own.

{ "title": "8. Brief synopsis", "items": [ "Possessive adjectives — before a noun: my, your, his, her, its, our, their.", "Possessive pronouns — instead of a noun: mine, yours, his, hers, ours, theirs.", "Is there a noun after? → Adjective (my book). No noun? → Pronoun (mine) It's = it is.", "Use the pattern in the examples.", "Use the pattern in the examples.", "In response to Whose? we use pronouns: mine, yours, his, hers, ours, theirs.", "Pronoun cannot stand with a noun: mine book (✗), my book (✓).", "Adjective cannot stand alone: ​​This is my (✗), This is mine (✓)." ] }
